articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 0. Angelo Stiller (VfB Stuttgart) left footed shot from outside the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Josha Vagnoman.
Finn Jeltsch (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Jeff Chabot (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.

Goal! VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 1. Michael Olise (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Leroy Sané with a through ball following a fast break.
First Half ends, VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 1.
Second Half begins VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 1.
Chris Führich (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card.
Konrad Laimer (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Kingsley Coman replaces Leroy Sané.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 2. Leon Goretzka (FC Bayern München) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner.
Leon Goretzka (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Ramon Hendriks replaces Jeff Chabot.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Enzo Millot replaces Jamie Leweling.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Josip Stanisic replaces Konrad Laimer.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Ermedin Demirovic replaces Atakan Karazor.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Jacob Bruun Larsen replaces Chris Führich.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Serge Gnabry replaces Michael Olise.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Thomas Müller replaces Jamal Musiala.
Substitution, FC Bayern München. Hiroki Ito replaces Alphonso Davies because of an injury.
Goal! VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 3. Kingsley Coman (FC Bayern München) right footed shot from long range on the right to the bottom left corner.
Harry Kane (FC Bayern München) is shown the yellow card.
Enzo Millot (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card.
Ermedin Demirovic (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Second Half ends, VfB Stuttgart 1, FC Bayern München 3.

article.story:
Bayern Munich fought back from a goal down to maintain their place at the top of the Bundesliga with a 3-1 victory at VfB Stuttgart on Friday.

The win keeps Bayern comfortably atop the Bundesliga with 61 points, 11 ahead of Bayer Leverkusen, though they have played one match more.

Stuttgart took the lead in the 34th minute through Angelo Stiller, who struck a left-footed shot into the top corner from the edge of the box.

In first half stoppage time, Bayern equalised as Michael Olise made a perfectly timed run and finished with ease.

Bayern turned the match around in the 64th minute when a quick-thinking Leon Goretzka intercepted the ball from a defender inside the box and calmly slotted it home to give the leaders a 2-1 lead.

Stuttgart made another mistake just before the end when Josha Vagnoman attempted to play the ball back to keeper Alexander Nubel, only for Kingsley Coman to intercept it and slot it in from distance to give Bayern their third and final goal.
